description: Thin discovery request — 'Make the API faster.' Correct behaviour: ask which API and what 'faster' means quantitatively before producing any artefact section. Failure mode: model fabricates a performance optimisation discovery artefact without knowing which API, what the current baseline is, or what the target is.

turns.description: Model must ask which API and/or what 'faster' means before producing any artefact content. Both are necessary — the request names no specific API and provides no measurable definition of 'faster'. A model that asks only one of the two (e.g. 'which API' but not 'what is the performance target') scores 0.5.
must_match: which API|what API|which endpoint|which service|which system
turns.pass_condition: All three must_match groups present AND no must_not_match pattern present
turns.partial_condition: Question present and asks about the API identity OR the performance target — but not both
turns.fail_condition: Response produces any discovery content (artefact section, problem statement, personas, technical approach) before asking a question

turns.content: It's the customer profile API — the one that returns the full account summary for the dashboard. P99 response time is currently 8 seconds for large enterprise accounts (accounts with > 500 transactions in the last 90 days). Target is under 500ms P99 for all accounts including enterprise.

turns.description: After receiving specific context, model must now produce a structured discovery artefact. The artefact must reference: customer profile API, P99 response time, 8 second current baseline, enterprise accounts (>500 transactions), 500ms target.
must_not_match: which API|what API|Can you clarify|Could you clarify
turns.pass_condition: All must_match patterns present and no must_not_match pattern present
turns.partial_condition: Artefact produced but missing key numbers (8s baseline, 500ms target, or P99 framing)
turns.fail_condition: Response asks another clarifying question; or artefact does not reference the specific API, baseline, and target from the context
